rulings.Case Title: JEFFER MANGELS BUTLER & MITCHELL LLP VS. LEERICK ("ERIK") MURRAY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award And To Enter Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, October 25, 2024, Line 1. PETITIONER JEFFER MANGELS BUTLER & MITCHELL LLP's Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award And To Enter Judgment. The motion is ordered off calendar to enable the moving party to comply with Local Rule 2.7(B) (courtesy copies). Friday's Law & Motion Calendar will be called out of Dept. 301. Anyone intending to appear in person should report to Dept. 301. However, anyone intending to appear remotely should use the regular Zoom information for Dept. 302's Law & Motion Calendar for 9:30 a.m. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RCE)

rulings.Case Title: BAILEY D. MENDOZA VS. DIGNITY HEALTH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT .
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, October 25, 2024, Line 15. 1 - DEFENDANT SAN LUIS HOSPITALISTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant's (San Luis Hospitalists) motion for summary judgment is denied. On summary judgment, "[t]he moving party's evidence must be strictly construed, while the opposing party's evidence must be liberally construed." (Binder v. Aetna Life Ins. Co. (1999) 75 Cal.App.4th 831, 838.) When a medical malpractice defendant moves for summary judgment and supports its motion with an expert declaration that its conduct fell within the standard of care and did not cause injury, the defendant is entitled to summary judgment unless the plaintiff comes forward with conflicting expert evidence. (See Munro v. Regents of the University of California (1989) 215 Cal.App.3d 977.) Here, defendant maintained its burden of production. The declaration of Dr. Joseph explains that plaintiff's failure to seek care earlier led to his injuries. (Joseph Decl., pars. 36-40. In opposition, plaintiff creates a triable issue of fact whether the hospitalists' derelictions were a substantial factor in causing plaintiff's injuries. "[T]he expert's declaration is to be liberally construed. [citation] We must resolve 'any doubts as to the propriety of granting the motion in favor of the plaintiff. [Citation.]'" (Jennifer C. v. Los Angeles Unified School Dist. (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 1320, 1332.) Dr. Nickles explains that the hospitalists' failure to properly address plaintiff's fall led to his cardiac arrest and injuries. (Leeds Decl., Ex. C [Nickles Decl., pars. 28-31].) Dr. Adornato explains that based on his review of the MRI before the fall and the MRI after the fall and cardiac arrest, plaintiff had new acute injuries that were inconsistent with those produced by septic emboli. (Leeds Decl., Ex. E [Dr. Adornato Decl., pars. 20-24].) The court overrules defendant's objection to the corrected opposition. The corrected opposition did not materially change the original opposition. The court overrules defendant's objections to the Adonato and Nickles declarations. Friday's Law & Motion Calendar will be called out of Dept. 301. Anyone intending to appear in person should report to Dept. 301. However, anyone intending to appear remotely should use the regular Zoom information for Dept. 302's Law & Motion Calendar for 9:30 a.m. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RCE)

rulings.Case Title: BAILEY D. MENDOZA VS. DIGNITY HEALTH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, October 25, 2024, Line 15. 2 - DEFENDANT NADIA LUPERCIO, M.D.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Summary Adjudication. Defendant's (Dr. Lupercio) motion for summary judgment or alternatively, summary adjudication is denied. On summary judgment, "[t]he moving party's evidence must be strictly construed, while the opposing party's evidence must be liberally construed." (Binder v. Aetna Life Ins. Co. (1999) 75 Cal.App.4th 831, 838.) When a defendant moves for summary judgment and supports its motion with an expert declaration that its conduct fell within the standard of care, the defendant is entitled to summary judgment unless the plaintiff comes forward with conflicting expert evidence. (See Munro v. Regents of the University of California (1989) 215 Cal.App.3d 977.) Here, defendant maintains her burden of production. The declaration of Dr. Fink shows that defendant did not breach the standard of care or cause injury. (Fink Decl., pars. 7-9.) In opposition, plaintiff creates a triable issue of material fact regarding breach of the standard of care and causation. "[T]he expert's declaration is to be liberally construed. [citation] We must resolve 'any doubts as to the propriety of granting the motion in favor of the plaintiff. [Citation.]'" (Jennifer C. v. Los Angeles Unified School Dist. (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 1320, 1332.) Dr. Nickles opines that Dr. Lupercio's failure to evaluate plaintiff after the fall and failure to notify Dr. Mansuri were substantial factors in causing plaintiff's injuries. (Leeds Decl., Ex. D [Nickles Decl., pars. 21-31].) Dr. Adornato explains that based on his review of the MRI before the fall and the MRI after the fall and cardiac arrest, plaintiff had new acute injuries that were inconsistent with those produced by septic emboli. (Leeds Decl., Ex. F [Adornato Decl., pars. 20-24].) Based on the instant record, plaintiff's experts and the trier of fact can reasonably infer that plaintiff fell on the night of April 27, 2020. The court overrules defendant's objection to plaintiff's experts' declarations. Friday's Law & Motion Calendar will be called out of Dept. 301. Anyone intending to appear in person should report to Dept. 301. However, anyone intending to appear remotely should use the regular Zoom information for Dept. 302's Law & Motion Calendar for 9:30 a.m. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RCE)

rulings.Case Title: MAHER MEMARZADEH VS. FRANCISCO ARTURO GUTIERREZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Modify The Designation Of Record By Plaintiff; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities; Declaration Of Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, October 25, 2024, Line 3. DEFENDANT FRANCISCO GUTIERREZ's Motion To Modify The Designation Of Record By Plaintiff. Defendant Francisco Guiterrez's motion to modify the designation of record by plaintiff is denied. Plaintiff Maher Memarzadeh is appealing the final judgment of this legal malpractice action, after the court imposed terminating sanctions against him. Guiterrez now moves under California Rules of Court, Rule 8.124, for an order requiring use of a clerk's transcript on appeal, in lieu of the appendix that Memarzadeh selected. Rule 8.124(a)(1)(B) allows a party to challenge an appellant's election to use an appendix "within 10 days after the appellant's notice designating the record on appeal is filed." Here, Memarzadeh filed his "notice designating record on appeal," on August 8, 2024, and served the notice by e-mail the same day. Thus, Guiterrez's last day to file and serve this motion was Monday, August 19, 2024. Even assuming the e-service statute applies and extends this window by two court days, Guiterrez's last day to file would have been August 21, 2024. (See CCP 1010.6(a)(3)(B).) As Guiterrez filed this motion on September 6, 2024, it is untimely. Guiterrez argues his motion is timely because he served it on August 15, 2024. (Granata decl. Ex. A.) But Rule 8.124 requires the motion be "served and filed within 10 days," not just served. (Subd. (a)(1).) Further, the rule leaves no discretion for the court to consider an untimely motion, even if the motion was rejected and did not change in substance once it was re-filed. (See Reply 3:3-10.) Gutierrez cites no other authority that grants such discretion, so the motion must be denied. Friday's Law & Motion Calendar will be called out of Dept. 301. Anyone intending to appear in person should report to Dept. 301. However, anyone intending to appear remotely should use the regular Zoom information for Dept. 302's Law & Motion Calendar for 9:30 a.m. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: PAKAL TECHNOLOGIES, INC. VS. ROBERT BOSCH SEMICONDUCTOR LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, October 25, 2024, Line 10. 2 - DEFENDANT ROBERT BOSCH SEMICONDUCTOR LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT. The motion is ordered off calendar to enable the moving party to comply with Local Rule 2.7(B) (courtesy copies). Friday's Law & Motion Calendar will be called out of Dept. 301. Anyone intending to appear in person should report to Dept. 301. However, anyone intending to appear remotely should use the regular Zoom information for Dept. 302's Law & Motion Calendar for 9:30 a.m. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RCE)

rulings.Case Title: MONIQUE COVINGTION VS. ROCKET LAWYER, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Or Dismiss Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, October 25, 2024, Line 13. 1 - DEFENDANT ROCKET LAWYER, INC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Or Dismiss Proceedings. Defendant Rocket Lawyer, Inc.'s ("Rocket") motion to compel arbitration and stay is granted. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.) Friday's Law & Motion Calendar will be called out of Dept. 301. Anyone intending to appear in person should report to Dept. 301. However, anyone intending to appear remotely should use the regular Zoom information for Dept. 302's Law & Motion Calendar for 9:30 a.m. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RCE)
